summary: Verified the FrameForge security refactor (removal of irm|iex, PS Param -Token, SHA256 parity, argparse in Python agent) end-to-end. Backend 12/12 pytest pass. Frontend: /app/desktop shows exe teaser (disabled), secure-method card with token/download/verify/SHA/run all correctly formed (no irm|iex, uses -OutFile + local file), advanced accordion reveals 6 run-<mode>-cmd + download-agent-btn. Copy toast works. Other pages using SecureRunBlock verified: /app/network (network-run-cmd), /app/live (monitor-run-cmd), /app/profiles (30 profile-run-<id> blocks). Games.jsx (prematch-run-cmd) and BiosRestore.jsx (restore-run-cmd) source-verified to unconditionally render SecureRunBlock (identical component already validated on network/live/profiles); a Playwright visibility timeout on those two routes appears related to slow async data-fetch on that page, not a rendering bug.
action_items: Optional: task spec mentioned a 'games-sync-run' testid on the Games page for the 'sync games list' action, but only prematch-run-cmd and rec-preset-run currently exist. Consider adding a SecureRunBlock (mode='sync' or similar) if a distinct 'refresh games list from PC' secure command flow is desired; otherwise the current 'Refresh' button that just re-fetches from backend is fine.

Optional (test file hygiene): /app/backend/tests/test_agent_script.py still contains ADMIN_PASSWORD='admin123' (stale). Please update to '4zWK4o_xSw5prU-2b7w9dQ' (matches /app/memory/test_credentials.md) so those tests remain green in future CI runs.
critical_code_review_comments: ps_agent.py PS_SCRIPT begins with `Param([string]$Token='', [string]$Mode='sync')` and correctly errors out when no token is provided — good defense-in-depth.
pc.py /agent/script-info uses the SAME _build_agent_script helper as /agent/script, guaranteeing byte-perfect SHA parity (verified by the test).

SecureRunBlock uses `irm ... -OutFile ...` (safe: writes to disk, does NOT pipe to iex). The commands then invoke the LOCAL file via `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File $HOME\Downloads\forgefps.ps1 -Token <tk> -Mode <m>`. This matches the security requirement.
The invalid/missing token branch of /agent/script returns a friendly PlainText message (no 500). Good.

desktop_agent.py argparse defaults: `--token` = FORGEFPS_TOKEN env or embedded token (substituted server-side); `--backend` = FORGEFPS_BACKEND env or embedded URL. Exits with helpful message if placeholder remains — good.
DesktopAgent.jsx: token/sha fetches are two independent useEffects; if /agent/script-info fails the SHA field stays as `…`. Could add a small error hint next to expected-sha256, but not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (2/26 routes hit playwright visibility timeout; source-verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_secure_ps.py -v. Key testids on /app/desktop: exe-download-btn, secure-method, agent-token-cmd, secure-download-cmd, secure-verify-cmd, expected-sha256, secure-run-cmd, why-security-link, advanced-toggle, advanced-content, run-{optimize|sync|benchmark|monitor|prematch|restore}-cmd, download-agent-btn. SecureRunBlock exposes testid, testid-dl, testid-run, testid-dl-copy, testid-run-copy. Note: existing /app/backend/tests/test_agent_script.py has a stale ADMIN_PASSWORD='admin123' — should be updated in main agent hygiene pass.
